items.title: 'I thought I was going to die' - Venezuelans describe earthquake panic
items.summary: Buildings were flattened in the capital Caracas, where voices have been heard calling from the rubble.

items.title: France, UK and Spain see record temperatures as heatwave grips western Europe
items.summary: Tens of millions of people are grappling with punishing temperatures, which have led to red heat alerts across the continent.

items.title: Trump asks Congress for billions for Iran war, after tension with Republicans
items.summary: But the budget faces an uphill battle as the president spars with some members of his own party over the issue.

items.title: Oil price falls to levels not seen since before Iran war
items.summary: Energy prices have been on a wild ride since Iran responded to US and Israeli attacks by effectively closing the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Philippines bans video game played by alleged high school shooter
items.summary: The rare school shooting that left three students killed and 20 others injured has shocked the country.

items.title: Police seal off key roads in Nairobi as Kenya braces for Gen Z protests
items.summary: Protesters demand justice for more than 80 people killed during the 2024 demonstrations and last year's anniversary protests.

items.title: Anthropic accuses Chinese rival Alibaba of illicitly extracting AI capabilities
items.summary: The firm alleged that Alibaba used fraudulent accounts to access data from its Claude AI model.

items.title: Independent MPs launch new Australian centrist party
items.summary: Zali Steggall and Allegra Spender say Community Strong Australia will offer citizens 'reason over rage'.

items.title: Clean sweep for Mamdani-backed candidates in New York's Democratic primary
items.summary: Brad Lander unseats Dan Goldman in a race that laid bare the city's divisions over the Israel-Gaza war.

items.title: Air conditioning creates political divide after France records hottest day
items.summary: France is being forced to re-think its longstanding reservations about air-conditioning as temperatures soar.

items.title: Biggest city in Crimea without power after Ukraine strikes
items.summary: Sevastopol's Moscow-installed governor warns there will be no electricity in some areas until the evening.

items.title: UN nuclear chief says inspectors will visit Iran sites as part of war deal
items.summary: Rafael Grossi says the IAEA is "working on modalities" but an Iranian  minister says such access would only be part of a final deal with the US.

items.title: The ICE raids are over, but some Minnesotans say they still live in fear
items.summary: Six months after he Trump administration's immigration crackdown ended, Minneapolis is still feeling the impact.

items.title: This Indian state is trying to ensure no one grows old alone
items.summary: Kerala is searching for new ways to care for its ageing population as migration leaves more elderly people living alone.

items.title: How to cope in a heatwave - according to you
items.summary: From what to wear to getting a restful night's sleep, you share your tips for beating the heat.

items.title: Find out which university degrees could earn you most across your lifetime
items.summary: New data suggests which university degrees have the highest and lowest financial returns over a lifetime.

items.title: The woman who fought for Pakistan's disappeared men now faces life in jail
items.summary: The Nobel Peace Prize nominee has spent years demanding answers about the missing in Balochistan province.

items.title: Watch: Moment earthquakes hit airport and buildings in Caracas
items.summary: Video filmed by BBC reporters, contributors and locals shows the aftermath, as frantic residents evacuate.

items.summary: France, Spain and Italy, have been hardest hit by the heatwave so far.

items.title: 'We're begging' - Savannah Guthrie pleads for help as details of her mother’s case emerge
items.summary: Savannah Guthrie spoke on NBC's Today show about a note sent to the media in February regarding her missing mother, Nancy Guthrie, which claimed she had died.

items.title: Why was JD Vance in a luxury Swiss resort for talks with Iran?
items.summary: BBC correspondent Jessica Parker explains some key points of the direct talks between the US vice-president and Iran.
